g.data-narration: Welcome to Coprocessor 15 and Caches. This topic covers essential ARM system control and memory optimization techniques.
g.text: ARM Processor System Control & Memory Hierarchy

g.data-narration: CP15 controls MMU and caches for precise memory management, essential for deterministic embedded system performance.
g.text: • CP15 provides system control: MMU, cache controllers, access permissions
• Caches bridge CPU speed gap with main memory for faster access
• Critical for real-time embedded applications requiring deterministic performance

g.data-narration: We will explore CP15 architecture, cache organization, maintenance operations, and practical examples.
Coprocessor 15 is the system control coprocessor in ARM, providing access to MMU, cache, and configuration registers.
g.text: Format: MCR p15, 0, Rd, CRn, CRm, opcode2

g.data-narration: MCR and MRC instructions access CP15 registers using specific opcode combinations for different operations.
g.text: MRC p15, 0, Rd, c0, c0, 0 ; Read CTR

g.data-narration: ARM processors use Harvard L1 caches (separate I-cache and D-cache) with optional unified L2 cache.
Cache size equals sets times associativity times line length. Line length is typically 32 or 64 bytes.
Three main cache operations: invalidate removes without writing back, clean writes dirty data, flush does both.
MMU page table entries contain TEX, C, B bits that control cacheability and buffering of memory regions.
g.text: MMU & Cache Interaction (TEX, C, B Bits)
TEX[2:0] - Type Extension | C - Cacheable | B - Bufferable
nG - Not Global | S - Shareable | AP - Access Permission
Strongly Ordered: Never cached (TEX=000, C=0, B=0)

g.data-narration: Write-through updates both cache and memory immediately. Write-back updates cache only, writes memory on eviction.
g.text: Write to both cache AND main memory
Simple, guarantees coherency, more memory traffic
Write to cache only, memory on eviction

g.data-narration: The Cache Type Register (CTR) provides cache characteristics. Bits 27-24 indicate data cache line length.
ICIALLU invalidates instruction cache after code updates. DSB and ISB barriers ensure completion and pipeline flush.
g.text: MCR p15, 0, r0, c7, c5, 0 ; ICIALLU - Invalidate entire I-cache
DSB ; Data Sync Barrier - ensure completion
ISB ; Instruction Sync Barrier - flush pipeline

g.data-narration: DCCIMVAC cleans and invalidates data cache by address. Essential for DMA transfers to ensure data coherency.
g.text: Example: Clean & Invalidate Data Cache by Address
Ensures dirty data written, cache cleared
loop: DCCIMVAC r0 ; Clean+invalidate by address
ADD r0, r0, #64 ; Next cache line

g.data-narration: Remember: Cache size formula, MCR/MRC format, and synchronization barriers DSB/ISB for cache operations.
g.text: MCR/MRC p15, 0, Rd, cN, cM, op2
